title: plectranthus aegyptiacus
intro: Plectranthus aegyptiacus, also known as Egyptian mint, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region and Africa. It possesses medicinal properties, particularly due to its high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Plectranthus aegyptiacus include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves are also used in cooking, adding a minty flavor to various dishes.
